title: Microsoft Abruptly Terminates VeraCrypt Account, Halting Windows Updates
summary: Updates to VeraCrypt, a popular and long-running piece of encryption, are now thrown into doubt because of a seemingly unexplained Microsoft decision.
reason: A dominant platform arbitrarily severing infrastructure that privacy-dependent users rely on illustrates how routing architecture for secure communication sits inside capturable chokepoints.
angle: Use the routing architecture tool to show how VeraCrypt's distribution through Microsoft's ecosystem reveals that even anti-surveillance tools are structurally dependent on the platforms they implicitly resist — the alibi being that open-source software is inherently free from platform capture.

angle: Use the routing architecture tool: these laws don't just protect companies, they rewire which bodies can hold capital accountable and which communities are structurally unable to seek redress — a jurisdictional enclosure of the legal commons.
